title: Crispy German Walnut Curry
description: A festive, nutty curry that blends crispy roasted walnuts, spicy Madras paste, and a creamy tomato base for a lowsodium German-inspired dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger; cook for another 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the Madras curry paste, smoked paprika, and tomato paste; fry for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the paste darkens slightly.
Pour in the low‑sodium vegetable stock and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the coconut milk and whisk to combine, then return to a simmer.
Fold in the toasted walnuts and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Taste and add a pinch of salt if needed; adjust consistency with a splash more stock if too thick.
Remove from heat, sprinkle chopped parsley over the top, and give a final gentle stir.
Serve hot over steamed rice, quinoa, or a side of crusty rye bread for an extra German touch.
